stem: A function q estimates that there were 5,000 water lilies on a pond in 2010. Each year from 2010 to 2025, the function estimates that the number of water lilies on the pond increased by 6% of the number of lilies in the pond the previous year. Which equation defines this function, where q(x) is the estimated number of water lilies on the pond x years after 2010?
rationale: Choice A is correct. A quantity that increases by a fixed percent each year is modeled by an exponential function of the form q(x) = I(1 + r)x, where I is the initial amount and r is the annual growth rate expressed as a decimal. Here I = 5,000 and r = 0.06, so q(x) = 5,000(1.06)x. Choice B models a 6% decrease. Choice C multiplies the previous value by 6 each year (a 500% increase). Choice D models a 60% annual increase.
